transliteration:
sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣṭāśma-kāñcanaḥ |
tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-saṃstutiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Equal in sorrow and happiness, self-abiding, regarding a clod, stone and gold alike; the same to dear and undear, firm, the same in blame and praise of himself.
contextual_meaning: A person who has transcended the three guṇas remains internally undisturbed by polarities—pain/pleasure, loss/gain, criticism/praise—because identity is anchored in the stable witness-self, not in shifting external valuations.
psychological_state: Post-autonomous ego: the mind no longer chases or avoids experiences; self-worth is decoupled from social feedback and sensory fortune.
trigger_scenario: You check your phone: one message is a job rejection, another is a friend’s angry text, and a third is a compliment. You notice your heart rate stay steady and your self-talk remain kind and factual.
anchor_text: Stay steady within, untouched by praise or pain.
